title: Finding a missing angle using the angle sum
problem: A triangle has two angles measuring $65°$ and $47°$. Find the size of the third angle.

steps.instruction: State the angle sum property of a triangle.
steps.working: The angles of any triangle add to $180°$.

steps.instruction: Subtract from $180°$ to find the missing angle.

title: Finding base angles in an isosceles triangle
problem: An isosceles triangle has an apex angle of $50°$. Find the size of each base angle.

steps.instruction: Recall that the two base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal. Let each base angle be $b$.
Write an equation using the angle sum of a triangle.

title: Finding angles using algebraic expressions
problem: A triangle has angles $(2x + 15)°$, $(x + 10)°$, and $(x - 5)°$. Find $x$ and state the size of each angle.

steps.instruction: Write an equation using the angle sum of a triangle.
Collect like terms on the left side.
Check: the three angles should add to $180°$.
